explanation: This is a direct replication of the first true positive command. It uses a common technique where regsvr32 is executed with an uncommon extension (`.jpg`) to bypass detection based on file extension filtering.
This is a direct replication of the second true positive command. It renames `regsvr32.exe` to evade signature-based detection and executes it with an uncommon extension (`.ext`).

This is a direct replication of the third true positive command. It uses a semicolon to append an uncommon extension (`not_an_ext`) to a legitimate `.dll` file, which may bypass detection based on file extension filtering.
This is a direct replication of the fourth true positive command. It uses a `.txt` extension appended to a `.dll` file to evade detection based on file extension filtering.
